Catalog Description

Special Topics in... Credit 1 to 4. Special topics in computer science that are new or

unique that are not covered in existing courses.

Additional Course Details

NA

Course Learning Outcomes

Upon completion of this course, the learner will be able to:

Create programs to solve problems using various AI tools for logical

inference, including unification, forward-chaining, backward-chaining,

and resolution.

Write programs in PROLOG to solve various types of problems, and

understand how negation-as-failure and the cut operator work.

Analyze the computational complexity of logical inference, and describe

algorithmic heuristics for improving the efficiency of inference.

Compare different methods for Default and Non-monotonic Reasoning,

and evaluate its utility in building real-world knowledge-based systems.

Solve problems using various AI tools for symbolic planning, including

forward state-space search, goal regression, and partial order planning.

Analyze the computational complexity of symbolic planning, and

describe algorithmic heuristics for improving the efficiency of

generating plans.

Evaluate methods for making probabilistic inferences with Bayesian

Networks, and for solving Markov Decision Problems.

Special Course Designation

None

NA

Grading Policy

Programming assignments: 80%.

Final project: 20%

The cutoff for an `A' will be >= 90% of total score, >= 80% for a `B', >= 70% for

a `C', >= 60% for a `D', and below 60% for an 'F'.

As a stacked class, the difference in work as a function of status of student is that

undergraduate students in CSCE 489 will do exercises using existing AI tools (such as MiniSat,

Prolog, Clips, etc.), whereas the graduate students in CSCE 689 will also implement some of

the underlying algorithms.

Late Work Policy

Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not

considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy ( Student Rule 7 ).

Course Specific Late Work Policy

Late penalty: -10% per day late (prorated over 24 hours).

Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not considered late

work and is exempted from the late work policy ( Student Rule 7 ).

Additional Course Information

AI Statement

This course assumes that all work submitted by students will be generated by the students

themselves. Students should not have another person/entity do the writing of any substantive

portion of an assignment for them, which includes hiring a person or a company to write

assignments and using artificial intelligence tools like ChatGPT or GitHub Copilot.

Course Specific Makeup Work Policy

Deadlines will be adjusted for students excused as per Student Rule 7.
